concept: Amend / Formally Change Part of a Continuing Matter
description: To formally change a stated part of a continuing rule, proposal, decision, procedure, record, agreement, role, or institutional arrangement while preserving its identifiable continuity and amendment history. Through the event-noun rule the word also names an amendment. Amending does not necessarily correct, improve, legitimize, enact, repeal, replace, conceal, or erase the prior version, and exact changed wording remains source material.
sound_symbolism: Changing 'helu' settles into continuing 'nari', altering a durable form without pretending that its history disappeared. The ending carries both revision and trace.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb with the amending agent as subject and the continuing matter as object: 'ha kowela ha nasholu helunari.' (The council amends this rule.) Preserve exact changed wording, version, date, authority, and effective conditions in a separate source record. The event-noun rule supplies amendment. Distinct from 'helui' (change), correction, replacement, repeal, implementation, and review.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Amendment treats institutional forms as conditioned and revisable while preserving the causal and ethical history of earlier versions.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Named amendment prevents authorities from presenting a changed rule as timeless or quietly erasing the wording under which people previously acted.

tags.activity: formally changing part of a continuing rule or record
